labels.analysis: The MCP Server "OpenClaw MCP Ecosystem" provides 5 tools for productivity tasks.
labels.reasoning: Primary label "Productivity" assigned based on tool names and descriptions.

tools.name: convert_timestamp
tools.description: Convert a timestamp between formats: unix epoch (seconds/ms) ↔ ISO 8601 ↔ human-readable ↔ relative ("3 hours ago"). Auto-detects input format. Returns all formats at once plus UTC offset info.

input.description: The timestamp to convert. Can be: unix epoch (e.g. 1714000000), ms epoch (e.g. 1714000000000), ISO 8601 (e.g. 2024-04-25T10:00:00Z), or human readable (e.g. "Apr 25, 2024 10:00 AM").

timezone.description: Timezone for human-readable output. Accepts IANA (e.g. "Asia/Tokyo") or abbreviation (e.g. "JST"). Default: "UTC".

tools.name: timezone_convert
tools.description: Convert a datetime from one timezone to another. When show_all=true, displays the time in 7 common timezones simultaneously (UTC, JST, EST, PST, CET, IST, CST).

datetime.description: Datetime to convert. ISO 8601 string or unix epoch number as string. E.g. "2024-04-25T10:00:00" or "1714000000".

from_tz.description: Source timezone. IANA name (e.g. "America/New_York") or abbreviation (e.g. "EST").

show_all.description: If true, show time in all 7 common timezones: UTC, JST, EST, PST, CET, IST, CST.

tools.name: parse_cron
tools.description: Parse a standard 5-field cron expression (minute hour day month weekday). Returns: human-readable description, validation status, next 5 run times. E.g. "*/5 9-17 * * 1-5" → "Every 5 minutes from 9am to 5pm, Mon-Fri".

expression.description: Cron expression with 5 fields: minute hour day month weekday. Supports *, /step, ranges (1-5), lists (1,3,5), and named months/days.

tools.name: time_diff
tools.description: Calculate the difference between two datetimes. Returns difference in seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks, and approximate months. Handles past/future direction automatically.

start.description: Start datetime. ISO 8601, unix epoch, or human readable string.

end.description: End datetime. ISO 8601, unix epoch, or human readable string. Defaults to "now" if omitted.

tools.name: format_duration
tools.description: Convert between duration formats: seconds (integer) ↔ human string ("2h 30m 15s") ↔ ISO 8601 duration ("PT2H30M15S"). Auto-detects input format.

input.description: Duration to convert. Can be: integer seconds (e.g. 9015), human string (e.g. "2h 30m 15s" or "1 day 4 hours"), or ISO 8601 duration (e.g. "PT2H30M15S" or "P1DT4H").
